creator: rtm@lcs.mit.edu
text:
Created attachment 242135
broken ffs image with negative jb_blk that can cause fsck to crash

I've attached a gzipped ffs image with a negative block number
in a journal record which causes ffs_isblock() to index into its cp[]
argument with a negative index. You may need valgrind to see the problem. A
backtrace from fsck_ffs -y fsck24a.img:

(gdb) where
#0  ffs_isblock (fs=<optimized out>, cp=0x800a370d8 "", h=-240) at /usr/src/sys/ufs/ffs/ffs_subr.c:922
#1  0x0000000000227b10 in blk_isfree (bno=-9204789740589546200) at suj.c:523
#2  0x000000000022781c in blk_isindir (blk=-9204789740589546200, ino=3, lbn=-4611686018427387913) at suj.c:377
#3  0x00000000002273eb in indir_visit (ino=3, lbn=-4611686018427387913, blk=-9204789740589546200, frags=0x7fffffffe668, visitor=0x229180 <blk_free_visit>, flags=1) at suj.c:728
#4  0x000000000022bb6e in blk_free_lbn (blk=-9204789740589546200, ino=3, lbn=-4611686018427387913, frags=8, follow=1) at suj.c:917
#5  0x000000000022b9c9 in blk_check (sblk=0x800a93030) at suj.c:1541
#6  0x0000000000227195 in cg_check_blk (sc=0x800a888c0) at suj.c:1612
#7  0x0000000000226dc5 in cg_apply (apply=0x227150 <cg_check_blk>) at suj.c:1638
#8  0x0000000000225571 in suj_check (filesys=0x7fffffffed71 "junk") at suj.c:2461
#9  0x00000000002195c6 in checkfilesys (filesys=0x7fffffffed71 "junk") at main.c:356
#10 0x0000000000218f72 in main (argc=1, argv=0x7fffffffea20) at main.c:210
